error_signature: can't deactivate, save, or archive workflows
title: Unable to deactivate or save workflows in n8n
symptom: Users are unable to deactivate, save, or archive their workflows after activation. This issue persists across all workflows.
not_this_if: The issue does not occur with newly created empty flows
root_cause: The problem arises due to insufficient file permissions for the settings file in the Docker container. When the permissions are not correctly set, n8n cannot modify the workflow state, leading to the inability to deactivate or save workflows.
fix_steps: Rebuild and restart your Docker container
agent_instruction: Add the environment variable to your Dockerfile and restart n8n.
